messages.content: You are BioGPT, an expert compiler architect. Translate the provided natural language biological protocol into valid, deterministic BioScript code for execution on a microfluidic biochip.

Combine 10 units of the diluted urine sample with 100 units of the benzodiazepine-enzyme conjugate. Mix for 60 seconds. Incubate the combined mixture at 23C (room temperature) for 60 minutes, allowing free benzodiazepine in the sample to compete with the enzyme-labeled conjugate for a limited number of antibody binding sites. Wash the reaction six times: each wash cycle combines 350 units of distilled water with the reaction droplet, mixes for 45 seconds, and removes unbound material. Add 100 units of TMB substrate to the washed reaction and mix for 60 seconds. Incubate at 23C for 30 minutes to allow the color reaction to develop. Add 100 units of stop solution and mix for 60 seconds to halt color development. Measure the resulting absorbance signal to obtain the reading, then discard the sample.

module absorbance

manifest urinesample
manifest diazepam_conjugate
manifest distilled_water
manifest tmb_substrate
manifest stop_reagent

instructions:

urine = dispense 10 units of urinesample
conjugate = dispense 100 units of diazepam_conjugate
a = mix urine with conjugate for 60s
heat a at 23c for 60m

repeat 6 times {
    water = dispense 350 units of distilled_water
    a = mix a with water for 45s
}

tmb = dispense 100 units of tmb_substrate
b = mix a with tmb for 60s
heat b at 23c for 30m

stop = dispense 100 units of stop_reagent
c = mix b with stop for 60s

diazepam_reading = detect absorbance on c for 1m
dispose c

metadata.notes: Exact numeric match across every value in the cited source: sample volume (10 units), conjugate volume (100 units), first incubation (23C/60m), wash count and volume (6x, 350 units), substrate volume (100 units), second incubation (23C/30m), stop volume (100 units). Only divergence: .bs uses BioScript's generic 'absorbance' detect module (module name itself is UNVERIFIED against the real compiler grammar - only 'fluorescence' and 'weight' are confirmed-working module names from repo ground truth; this needs a compile test) in place of the source's literal dual-wavelength (450nm/650nm) absorbance reading - a known BioScript expressiveness gap, not a transcription error. Scope caveat: the cited kit is raised against Oxazepam with cross-reactivity across the benzodiazepine class (including diazepam), not diazepam-exclusive; this does not affect the validity of the matched procedure. This .bs is a CORRECTED version of the original repo file: the original included a second antibody/HRP-conjugate incubation+wash stage with no real-world small-molecule (hapten) immunoassay precedent (two-step/sandwich ELISA format is not physically achievable for low-molecular-weight drug antigens), and had a structural defect where the actual urine/antibody reaction was disposed without ever reaching the substrate step (final reading was disconnected from the input sample). The two-step structure was removed as non-representative; this single-step version is the corrected, source-matched replacement. Pending 3-LLM cross-check before validated:true; pending compiler confirmation of 'module absorbance' syntax validity.

chemical_evidence.source_quote: A 10 μL aliquot of a diluted unknown specimen
incubated with a 100 μL dilution of enzyme...labeled Benzodiazepine derivative
Add 100 μL of Substrate reagent to each well
Add 100 μL of Stop Solution to each well

operation_evidence.source_quote: Incubate for 60 minutes at room temperature (20-25°C)

operation_evidence.bs_operation: repeat 6 times { mix 350 units water for 45s }
operation_evidence.source_quote: Wash the wells 6 times with 350 μL of distilled water
Incubate for 30 minutes at room temperature
Measure the absorbance at a dual wavelength of 450 nm and 650 nm

numeric_mismatches.reason: BioScript has no dual-wavelength absorbance instruction; documented expressiveness gap, not an error
